programs.name: Home Care for the Elderly (HCE) Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides a basic subsidy to support family-type living arrangements for seniors age 60 and older within private homes. This state-funded program helps elders remain in community settings rather than institutional care.
programs.who_its_for: Florida residents aged 60+ in family-type living arrangements with income and asset limits.
programs.whats_unique: Florida-specific state-funded subsidy unique to private home care arrangements, not a federal program.
programs.benefit_value: Basic monthly subsidy (amount varies by income, up to ~$160/month based on state guidelines)

programs.name: Community Care for the Elderly (CCE) Program
programs.what_it_does: Delivers community-based services including medical supplies, emergency alert response, and therapeutic services to functionally impaired elders. Services are organized to enable living in the least restrictive, cost-effective environment.
programs.who_its_for: Functionally impaired Florida elders needing community-based support, based on assessed needs, income, and residency.
programs.whats_unique: Florida's state-administered non-Medicaid program providing a continuum of care services tailored to avoid institutionalization.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by service package; can include in-home care, meals, and supplies worth thousands annually

programs.name: Respite for Elders Living in Everyday Families (RELIEF) Program
programs.what_it_does: Offers in-home respite care to give family caregivers breaks from responsibilities for homebound elderly individuals. This increases the family unit's ability to continue providing care at home.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of homebound Florida elders, with eligibility based on elder's age, frailty, and family income.
programs.whats_unique: State-funded respite specifically for everyday family caregivers in Florida, distinct from federal OAA allotments.
programs.benefit_value: In-home respite hours (typically 20-360 hours/year depending on priority level)

programs.name: Optional State Supplementation (OSS) for SSI Recipients
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly cash supplements to SSI recipients in assisted living or adult family care homes. Supports basic expenses for qualifying seniors in residential care facilities.
programs.who_its_for: SSI-eligible Florida seniors in adult family care homes or assisted living facilities meeting federal resource limits.
programs.whats_unique: Florida's state-level supplement to federal SSI, optional and targeted at specific residential care settings (unlike states without OSS).

programs.name: Additional Homestead Exemption for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Offers supplemental property tax exemption beyond the standard $25,000 homestead for qualifying seniors. Reduces taxable property value by up to $50,000 in some areas, saving on annual taxes.
programs.who_its_for: Florida homeowners aged 65+ meeting income thresholds (varies by county, e.g., $35,000 or less).
programs.whats_unique: State-authorized extra exemption stacking on federal homestead, with local variations like $50,000 in Tampa—beyond standard federal tax relief.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $50,000 exemption; ~$600-$2,400 annual tax savings depending on millage rate

programs.name: SHINE (Serving Health Insurance Needs of Elders)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free, unbiased one-on-one counseling on Medicare, Medicaid, and health insurance options. Empowers seniors through volunteer-assisted guidance to select optimal coverage.
programs.who_its_for: All Florida elders (primarily 65+) seeking health insurance counseling, no income restrictions.
programs.whats_unique: Florida Department of Elder Affairs' statewide volunteer network for personalized insurance help, not replicated identically in every state.
programs.benefit_value: Free counseling; potential savings of thousands via optimized Medicare/Part D plans
